perturbation.original_text: "Data" means all data, databases and collections and compilations of data, in any form or medium.
perturbation.changed_text: "Data" means all data, databases and collections and compilations of data, in any form or medium, **specifically excluding metadata related to user behavior analysis for marketing purposes.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definition of 'Data'. In Section 1.01, 'Data' is broadly defined, encompassing all forms and mediums. However, the added exclusion narrows this definition, creating an ambiguity. It introduces an in-text contradiction with other clauses using a broader definition of Data, specifically contradicting Section 5.05 which refers to Intellectual Property Rights-related documents, materials or other information or technology which can include metadata and user bahavior.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly set forth in this Agreement, in the Separation Agreement or any other mutually executed agreement between the Parties or any of the members of their respective Groups, no Party or any member of its Group shall be required to provide the other Party with any technical assistance or to furnish any other Party with, or obtain on their behalf, any Intellectual Property Rights-related documents, materials or other information or technology.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'Data' introduces an exclusion of 'metadata related to user behavior analysis for marketing purposes'. This contradicts Section 5.05, which refers to 'Intellectual Property Rights-related documents, materials or other information or technology'. Since metadata can be considered 'information or technology' and related to intellectual property, the exclusion in the definition of 'Data' directly conflicts with the broader scope of information covered in Section 5.05, creating an in-text contradiction as it is no longer clear whether metadata is covered under the obligations in section 5.05.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as of the Distribution Date, Nuance hereby grants to SpinCo and the members of the SpinCo Group a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, perpetual and irrevocable, transferable (subject to ARTICLE VIII), sublicensable (subject to Section 3.01(g)) license under the Nuance Patents, solely to the extent that claims of the Nuance Patents cover products or services of the SpinCo Business in the SpinCo Field of Use, together with natural extensions and evolutions thereof, in each case to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise exploit such products and services, together with natural extensions and evolutions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as of the Distribution Date, Nuance hereby grants to SpinCo and the members of the SpinCo Group a worldwide, non-exclusive, fully paid-up, license under the Nuance Patents, solely to the extent that claims of the Nuance Patents cover products or services of the SpinCo Business in the SpinCo Field of Use, together with natural extensions and evolutions thereof, in each case to make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale, import and otherwise exploit such products and services, together with natural extensions and evolutions thereof. **This license is valid for a period of five years from the Distribution Date, and is not transferable without Nuance's explicit written consent.**
perturbation.explanation: The modification alters the license granted by Nuance to SpinCo, creating a contradiction. The original text specifies a perpetual and irrevocable license, whereas the modified text imposes a five-year limit and restricts transferability. This directly contradicts the initial perpetual and irrevocable nature, causing an in-text contradiction. It now creates a conflicting interpretation of section 8.01 where transferability is mentioned
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly set forth in this Agreement, neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, interests or obligations under this Agreement, including the licenses granted pursuant to this Agreement, shall be assigned, in whole or in part, by operation of Law or otherwise by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Any purported assignment without such consent shall be void. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if any Party to this Agreement (or any of its successors or permitted assigns) (a) shall enter into a consolidation or merger transaction in which such Party is not the surviving entity and the surviving entity acquires or assumes all or substantially all of such Party's assets, (b) shall transfer all or substantially all of such Party's assets to any Person or (c) shall assign this Agreement to such Party's Affiliates, then, in each such case, the assigning Party (or its successors or permitted assigns, as applicable) shall ensure that the assignee or successor-in-interest expressly assumes in writing all of the obligations of the assigning Party under this Agreement, and the assigning Party shall not be required to seek consent, but shall provide written notice and evidence of such assignment, assumption or succession to the non-assigning Party. Subject to the preceding sentences, this Agreement will be binding upon, inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by, the Parties and their respective successors and assigns. No assignment permitted by this Section 8.01 shall release the assigning Party from liability for the full performance of its obligations under this Agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, in no event will the licenses granted in this Agreement extend to products, services or other activities of the assignee existing on or before the date of the transaction described in clauses (a) or (b) of the preceding sentence, except to the extent that they were licensed under the terms of this Agreement prior to such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a five-year limit and restricts transferability of the license, directly contradicting the original text's specification of a 'perpetual and irrevocable' license and conflicting with Section 8.01 which addresses transferability. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text discusses assignment of the agreement, including licenses granted pursuant to it, implying a potentially longer duration and different conditions for transfer than the 5-year, non-transferable term introduced in the modified text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that it shall comply with all applicable national and international laws and regulations relating to import and/or export control in its country(ies), if any, involving any commodities, software, services or technology within the scope of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it shall comply with all applicable national and international laws and regulations relating to import and/or export control in its country(ies), if any, involving any commodities, software, services or technology within the scope of this Agreement. **However, compliance with these regulations is not required if it hinders the party's ability to meet market demands or gain a competitive advantage.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence introduces an exception to the compliance obligation. It creates a contradiction because, while the first sentence mandates adherence to all import/export laws, the second sentence allows non-compliance under certain conditions, making the obligation uncertain and unenforceable, making the text contradictory with 11.01 section that says all agreement should be valid and enforceable in accordance with the terms thereof
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Nuance represents on behalf of itself and each other member of the Nuance Group, and SpinCo represents on behalf of itself and each other member of the SpinCo Group, as follows: (i) each such Person has the requisite corporate or other power and authority and has taken all corporate or other action necessary in order to execute, deliver and perform this Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby; and (ii) this Agreement has been duly executed and delivered by it and constitutes, or will constitute, a valid and binding agreement of it enforceable in accordance with the terms thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the compliance obligation stated in the original text. The original text states that 'Each Party agrees that it shall comply with all applicable national and international laws and regulations relating to import and/or export control'. The changed text adds 'However, compliance with these regulations is not required if it hinders the party's ability to meet market demands or gain a competitive advantage.' This directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text that the agreement will constitute a 'valid and binding agreement of it enforceable in accordance with the terms thereof.' The exception makes the obligation to comply with laws optional, rendering the agreement less 'valid and binding,' making it a strong contradiction.
